examples.name: 1. an exact path
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/health
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/health
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: 2. a wildcard segment (*)
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/*/health
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/health
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: 3. everything below (**)
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/**
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/users/123
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: 4. capture a segment, check a trait
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/projects/{project}/**
    methods:
      - GET
    where: contains(user.traits["allowed_projects"], vars.project)
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/alpha/issues
identity:
    name: alice
    roles:
        - developer
    traits:
        allowed_projects:
            - alpha
            - beta

examples.name: 5. the whole model in one tree
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources_expression:
  # allows /api/v4/projects/g%2fp/issues
  # denies /api/v4/projects/g%2fp/secret
  - |-
    path.match(literal("api/v4/projects",
        capture_encoded("project", set("/"),
            greedy_without("secret"))),
    allow_encoded(set("/")))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/mygroup%2fmyproject/issues
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: exact
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/health
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/health
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: multiple exact
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/health
      - /api/v4/status
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/status
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: single segment *
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/*/health
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/health
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: greedy **
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/**
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/users/123
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: multiple methods
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/**
    methods:
      - GET
      - HEAD
examples.input:
request:
    method: HEAD
    path: /api/users
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: wrong method denied
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/**
    methods:
      - GET
examples.input:
request:
    method: POST
    path: /api/users
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: checked against a set
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /data/*/{letter}/**
    where: contains(set("a", "b", "c"), vars.letter)
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /data/x/b/info
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: checked against a trait
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/projects/{project}/**
    methods:
      - GET
    where: contains(user.traits["allowed_projects"], vars.project)
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/alpha/issues
identity:
    name: alice
    roles:
        - developer
    traits:
        allowed_projects:
            - alpha
            - beta

examples.name: equals identity
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/user/{username}
    where: user.name == vars.username
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/user/alice
identity:
    name: alice
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: slash: a trailing slash is required
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /files/
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /files
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: optional: match with or without a trailing slash
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources_expression:
  - |-
    path.match(literal("files",
        optional(slash())))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /files/
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: optional: an optional trailing subtree
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources_expression:
  # The path may end at files, or carry
  # the optional reports tail, from one
  # tree with no duplicated prefix.
  - |-
    path.match(literal("files",
        optional(literal("reports"))))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /files
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: path, method, and trait
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /teams/{team}/**
    methods:
      - GET
    where: contains(user.traits["teams"], vars.team)
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /teams/security/dashboards
identity:
    name: alice
    roles:
        - developer
    traits:
        teams:
            - security
            - devs

examples.name: additive list (two rules)
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /public/**
  - paths:
      - /static/**
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /static/css/app.css
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: carve-out (paths grant, negated subtree)
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources_expression:
  # Allow all of /files, then carve out the
  # /files/secret subtree with a negated
  # path.match. The grant and the carve-out
  # share one expression, since path matching
  # never lives in a sugared where.
  - |-
    path.match(literal("files", greedy())) &&
    !path.match(literal("files",
        literal("secret", greedy())))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /files/a
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: anti-pattern (commented out)
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/projects/{project}/**
      # - /api/status
      #   {project} not bound for this path
    where: contains(user.traits["allowed_projects"], vars.project)
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/alpha/issues
identity:
    name: alice
    roles:
        - developer
    traits:
        allowed_projects:
            - alpha
            - beta

examples.name: glob_without: exclude single segments
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources_expression:
  - |-
    path.match(literal("files",
        glob_without(set("private", "secret"))))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /files/report
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: greedy_without: exclude and carve out subtrees
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources_expression:
  - |-
    path.match(literal("files",
        greedy_without("secret", "private")))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /files/reports/q3
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: greedy_except: exact segment, subtree still allowed
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources_expression:
  - |-
    path.match(literal("files",
        greedy_except(literal("secret"))))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /files/secret/notes
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: greedy_except: add greedy() to exclude the whole subtree
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources_expression:
  - |-
    path.match(literal("files",
        greedy_except(literal("secret", greedy()))))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /files/secret/key
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: root: several first segments in one tree
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources_expression:
  - |-
    path.match(root(
        literal("api", greedy()),
        literal("admin", greedy())))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /admin/settings
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: root: a path under no root is denied
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources_expression:
  - |-
    path.match(root(
        literal("api", greedy()),
        literal("admin", greedy())))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /public/x
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: root: nested anywhere but the top is a load error
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources_expression:
  - |-
    path.match(literal("files",
        root(literal("a"), literal("b"))))

examples.name: an encoded node without allow_encoded fails closed
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources_expression:
  # capture_encoded names an encoded
  # segment; without allow_encoded it
  # fails closed.
  # denies /registry/@babel%2fcore
  # allows /registry/lodash
  - |-
    path.match(literal("registry",
        capture_encoded("package", set("/"))))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /registry/@babel%2fcore
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: npm scoped package keeps its literal "@" raw
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources_expression:
  # allow_encoded now opts the match in.
  # capture_encoded binds /@scope%2fname
  # raw, so "@" stays "@", never %40.
  # allows /registry/@babel%2fcore
  - |-
    path.match(literal("registry",
        capture_encoded("package", set("/"))),
    allow_encoded(set("/")))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /registry/@babel%2fcore
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: GitLab project id, plain or encoded, in one node
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources_expression:
  # One capture_encoded binds a plain id or
  # an encoded group/project in one node.
  # allows project 123
  # allows project mygroup%2Fmyproject
  - |-
    path.match(literal("api/v4/projects",
        capture_encoded("project", set("/"), greedy())),
    allow_encoded(set("/")))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/123/issues
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: encoded_literal pins one group/project by its decoded value
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources_expression:
  # encoded_literal matches one segment by
  # its decoded value, in either hex case
  # (%2F or %2f).
  # allows project mygroup%2Fmyproject
  # denies project other%2Fthing
  - |-
    path.match(literal("api/v4/projects",
        encoded_literal("mygroup/myproject", set("/"),
            literal("issues"))),
    allow_encoded(set("/")))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/mygroup%2Fmyproject/issues
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: an encoded id, a carved-out subtree, and the encoded-slash opt-in
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources_expression:
  # allows /api/v4/projects/g%2fp/issues
  # denies /api/v4/projects/g%2fp/secret
  - |-
    path.match(literal("api/v4/projects",
        capture_encoded("project", set("/"),
            greedy_without("secret"))),
    allow_encoded(set("/")))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/mygroup%2fmyproject/issues
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: allow code and reason
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/projects/{project}/**
    where: contains(user.traits["allowed_projects"], vars.project)
    allow_code: allowed_project
    allow_reason: User has access to project
    deny_code: denied_project
    deny_reason: User has NO access to project
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/alpha/issues
identity:
    name: alice
    roles:
        - developer
    traits:
        allowed_projects:
            - alpha
            - beta

examples.name: deny hint: path matched, where failed
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/projects/{project}/**
    where: contains(user.traits["allowed_projects"], vars.project)
    allow_code: allowed_project
    allow_reason: User has access to project
    # deny_code is reported on the near-miss: the path and
    # method matched but the where failed.
    deny_code: denied_project
    deny_reason: User has NO access to project
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/gamma/issues
identity:
    name: alice
    roles:
        - developer
    traits:
        allowed_projects:
            - alpha
            - beta

examples.name: deny hints across rules, only some fire
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/projects/{project}/**
    where: contains(user.traits["allowed_projects"], vars.project)
    deny_code: denied_project
    deny_reason: Not allowed on this project
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/admin/**
    where: contains(user.roles, "admin")
    deny_code: denied_admin
    deny_reason: Admin role required
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/gamma/issues
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer
    traits:
        allowed_projects:
            - alpha

examples.name: no matching role
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
# The user must hold this role for
# its app_resources to apply.
app_resources:
  - paths: ["/api/v4/health"]
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/v4/health
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [auditor]
